name: Pan-Broiled Steak
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup red wine, brandy, beef stock or (as a last resort) water
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from the steak and reserve.
2. Sprinkle each side of the steak with salt and pepper.
3. Let the steak stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
4. Heat a heavy cast iron skillet over high heat.
5. Grease the skillet with the reserved beef fat.
6. Sear the steak quickly on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
7. Reduce heat to medium and continue cooking for an additional 2 to 3 minutes per side until desired degree of doneness is reached (use a meat thermometer: 125-130°F for medium-rare).
8. Remove the steak to a warm platter and keep it warm in the oven.
9. Discard all but 1 tablespoon of fat from the skillet.
10. Put the pan back over moderate heat and add the liquid.
11. When the liquid boils, scrape the pan with a wooden spoon to loosen the flavorful bits stuck to the bottom.
12. Continue cooking until the liquid is reduced in volume and slightly thickened.
13. Stir in butter.
14. Pour the sauce over the steak and serve. Makes 1 to 3 servings. Per 4-ounce serving: Calories 463, Fat 36g, Cholesterol 126 mg, Sodium 325 mg, Percent calories from fat 71.
